calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Probate Of Will
Off Calendar Jun-13-2012 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Todd Banks (Petitioner in Pro Per). Craig Banks (Contestant in Pro Per).

Craig Banks stated that Todd Banks has not paid the mortgage and the home will be lost. He further stated that Todd claimed the $38,000.00 bank account was stolen by Todd's attorney and the trust was never funded. With nothing to probate, the Court took the Petition for Probate off calendar without prejudice.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For Status Of Discovery And For A Referral To The Trial Department.
Off Calendar Jun-13-2012 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Probate Of Will
Off Calendar Apr-04-2012 Continued To Jun-13-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Sellars for and with Craig Banks.

Mr. Sellars stated that he will be filing a Subsitution of Attorney today and that Mr. Banks will need time to get new counsel. Mr. Banks reported that his brother is not in court and he cannot find him. The matter was continued to June 13, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

The Court noted that Todd Banks and Robert Thomas were not present. Craig Banks stated that he was searching for a new Attorney. The Petition for Probate of Will and status of discovery were continued to April 4, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Banks to obtain Counsel.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For Status Of Discovery And For A Referral To The Trial Department.
Off Calendar Mar-30-2010 Continued To May-10-10 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Sellers for Craig Banks.

The Court inquired how the mortgage would be paid. Mr. Sellers stated that the parties would contact Bank of America to see if they would pay the lender directly, that he did send a letter, that the bank informed him they would need a Court order, and that his client would stipulate to an order. In addition, in regard to discovery he stated that Ms. Victor was adament that he request it, that he did, and that he got no response; hence, the motion that the Court has before it for setting. The Court set the date on Craig Banks' Motion For Order Compelling Discovery Responses And For Sanctions for May 10, 2010 at 1:30 p.m. and stated that the matter would also be on for trial setting that date (referral to D-206).

Judge: Mary Wiss. Court Reporter: Nici Rodich, CSR #11604. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Probate Of Will
Off Calendar Mar-30-2010 Continued To May-10-10 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Jan-26-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Linda Williams (Trustee). Robert Sellards for Craig Banks. Dolores Victor for Todd Banks.

Ms. Victor reported that mediation was unsuccessful, stated that the mortgage was four months in arrears and requested that the either Todd Banks or the Trustee be allowed to pay the mortgage. Mr. Sellards agreed that the mortgage needed to be paid, stated that Todd Banks did not have access to the bank account and that discovery needed to be conducted. Counsel will work out the manner in which the mortgage is to be paid. The matter was set for March 30, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery and for a referral to the trial department.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR #2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Dec-01-2009 Continued To Jan-26-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dolores Victor for Todd Banks. Robert Sellards for Craig Banks.

Ms. Victor stated that mediation had been scheduled for November 19, 2009 but that opposing Counsel cancelled at the last minute and requested that a settlement conference be set. Mr. Sellards explained that he had to cancel because he had a trial on another matter at the same time. The Court encouraged Counsel to reschedule mediation. Mediation is to be completed by January 22, 2010. The matter was continued to January 26, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Notice Of Motion By Contestant Craig Banks For Reconsideration Of And/Or Relief From Order For Probate
Off Calendar Oct-08-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dolores Victor for and with Todd Banks. Robert Sellards for and with Craig Banks.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court encouraged the Parties to think about resolving the matter and to consider mediation. The Court granted the Motion for Reconsideration and set aside the order for probate signed on July 27, 2009. Mr. Sellards will prepare the order. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. Mediation is to be completed no later than November 24, 2009. Status on mediation is set for December 1, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 (order for the same signed in open court). If the matter does not settle, a neutral fiduciary may need to be appointed.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-27-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dolores Victor for and with Todd Banks. Craig Banks in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that this was a pre-granted matter and that no opposition had been filed. Craig Banks stated that he filed his contest on Friday. Ms. Victor objected to any argument by Craig Banks, stated that he was already given the opportunity to a file an objection/opposition, that the matter had been previously continued and that he had two months in which to do so. In addition, Ms. Victor stated that the mortgage was due and three months behind. Craig Banks stated that he did not object to the mortgage being paid, but that he was objecting to his Brother being appointed, the manner in which the will was put together and the manner in which the bank accounts were being used. The Court allowed the order to stand. The petition was granted.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Janet Pond, CSR#5292. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-13-2009 Continued To Jul-27-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Ex Parte Petition For Letters Of Special Administration
Off Calendar Jun-23-2009 Denied
